news.Title: This Vanguard Index Fund Could Turn $500 Per Month Into a $1.4 Million Portfolio as the AI Boom Unfolds
news.Content: The median annual income for full-time workers aged 25 to 34 was $59,280 during the first quarter, according to the Labor Department. That means after-tax earnings would be about $45,175 in the worst-case scenario. Financial planners usually recommend saving 20% of post-tax earnings for retirement, which would be about $9,000 per year (or $750 per month) for the median worker. Even a percentage of that figure ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF (VOOG) could potentially grow a $500 monthly investment into $1.4 million over 30 years, assuming a 12% annual return.
• The ETF's portfolio is heavily concentrated in technology and communications sectors, benefiting from trends like cloud and mobile adoption.
• The AI boom is expected to be a significant catalyst for the ETF, potentially driving returns comparable to or exceeding those from past tech trends.
• Key holdings include Nvidia (14.6%), Alphabet (11.1%), and Microsoft (9.5%), positioning the ETF to capitalize on the AI growth narrative.
news.Keywords: AI, ETF, technology, growth, investment

Management highlighted expanding AI/data‑center momentum with a multi‑year, multi‑billion engagement with AWS , start of high‑volume silicon photonics production, and a target of > $500M data‑center revenue in 2026 and > $1B in 2027. ST said bookings remain strong (book‑to‑bill above one) and distributor inventories are normalized, guiding Q2 revenue $3.45 billion ±350 bps and gross margin around 34.8%, supporting its view ...
news.AI_Summary:
• STMicroelectronics (STM) reported Q1 2026 net revenues of $3.1 billion, including $40 million from the acquired NXP MEMS sensor business.
• The company highlighted expanding AI/data-center momentum with a multi-year, multi-billion engagement with AWS and the start of high-volume silicon photonics production.

news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla Inc. has started manufacturing its Cybercab robotaxi, a key part of its Robotaxi network vision.
• Production will ramp slowly initially, with expectations for a more noticeable increase later this year and into next year.
• The Cybercab is envisioned as a purpose-built robotaxi, potentially more affordable than other Tesla models, but regulatory exemptions are needed for mass production without steering wheels or pedals.
• Tesla's ride-hailing business is expanding to new cities, but the ramp-up remains slow, with material revenue not expected until at least 2027.

news.Title: The Iran War May End Soon. The Damage to the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chip Supply Chain Has Already Been Done.
news.Content: Even if the ceasefire holds and diplomats find a way to resolve the mess in Iran, one thing is already true: The artificial intelligence (AI) chip supply chain will never look at the Middle East the same way again. As Vice President JD Vance led a U.S. delegation to Pakistan for negotiations this week -- with Iran's parliament speaker simultaneously warning of "new cards on the battlefield" -- the fragile two-week ceasefire that ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Iran conflict has highlighted critical vulnerabilities in the AI chip supply chain, particularly regarding helium supply from Qatar, essential for semiconductor manufacturing.
• Damage to Qatar's LNG infrastructure has halted operations and doubled helium spot prices, impacting chipmakers reliant on this resource.
• Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron may face margin compression due to helium supply disruptions, affecting their ability to supply Nvidia, Microsoft, and other AI companies.
• TSMC, a crucial foundry for Nvidia's advanced AI chips, is also at risk due to Taiwan's high energy import dependency and limited LNG reserves.
news.Keywords: AI, chip supply chain, helium, semiconductor, geopolitical risk

news.Title: Prediction: The Best-Performing Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock on the Nasdaq by Year-End Won't Be Nvidia
news.Content: In recent years, Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has demonstrated its ability to deliver explosive growth in both earnings and stock performance. The company is the leading maker of something that's been in extremely high demand: the artificial intelligence (AI) chip. Nvidia's graphics processing units (GPUs) are the compute powering the most critical of AI tasks, such as the training and inference of models. Though others sell AI chips, customers have ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Broadcom (AVGO) is predicted to be the best-performing AI stock on the Nasdaq by year-end, potentially outperforming Nvidia.
• Broadcom's AI chips (XPUs) are custom-designed for specific purposes, complementing Nvidia's general-purpose GPUs, allowing it to carve out market share without direct rivalry.
• The company forecasts over $100 billion in revenue from AI chips alone by 2027 and has verified its supply chain readiness.
• Major data center customers like Meta and Alphabet are increasing orders for Broadcom's XPUs, indicating deep, strategic, multiyear relationships.
news.Keywords: AI, semiconductor, growth, custom silicon, partnership

news.Title: Forget Innovation! Tim Cook's Greatest Contribution to Apple Is This $841 Billion Acquisition.

news.Title: Starbucks Reveals Groundbreaking Artificial Intelligence (AI) Platform. Will It Finally Recover?
news.Content: Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX) stock has disappointed shareholders over the past few years, but it's making strong efforts to turn that around. It's on its fourth CEO over the past five years, and it's making all kinds of changes to its business. Last week, it announced a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Starbucks is integrating its platform with ChatGPT to enhance customer experience, allowing AI to suggest beverages based on user preferences.
• The company reported Q1 fiscal 2026 revenue growth of 6% year-over-year, with comparable sales up 4%, indicating some progress despite sluggish growth in recent years.
• Operating margin was lower than the prior year, but the new CEO is confident in a profit turnaround.
• Starbucks opened 128 net new stores in Q1 fiscal 2026, bringing the total to 41,000 stores globally.
• The stock has risen 17% year-to-date, reflecting increased market confidence in the company's recovery efforts.
news.Keywords: AI integration, ChatGPT, revenue growth, comparable sales, store expansion, market confidence

news.Title: Moody's Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
news.Content: Key Points Moody’s reported a “strong start” to 2026 with both MIS and MA revenue up 8% , adjusted operating margin expanding 150 bps to 53.2% , adjusted diluted EPS of $4.33 (+13%), and $1.7 billion returned to shareholders including nearly $1.5 billion of buybacks as repurchase guidance was raised to about $2.5 billion. Moody’s Analytics is shifting to a subscription-first model with recurring revenue representing 98% of MA revenue and ARR at $3.6 billion (+8% YoY), while transactional revenue fell 54% due to a divestiture and strategic pullback and is expected to continue declining through 2026–27. Moody’s Investors Service ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Moody's reported a strong Q1 2026 with 8% revenue growth in both MIS and MA segments, and adjusted operating margin expansion to 53.2%.
• Adjusted diluted EPS increased by 13% to $4.33, and the company returned $1.7 billion to shareholders, including $1.5 billion in buybacks.
• Moody's Analytics (MA) shifted to a subscription-first model, with recurring revenue at 98% of MA revenue and Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) at $3.6 billion (+8% YoY).
• Moody's Investors Service (MIS) achieved record issuance topping $2 trillion with a 66.7% margin.
• The firm is expanding AI and digital finance initiatives, integrating content into enterprise AI platforms and publishing a stablecoin rating methodology.
news.Keywords: earnings, revenue growth, margin expansion, EPS, share repurchases, recurring revenue, ARR, AI initiatives

news.Title: 台積電這顆「大西瓜」太補？台經院孫明德憂：把小西瓜養分都吸走了
news.Content: 台股今（24）日盤中爆漲逾1200點，史上盤中第六大，指數挑戰3萬9大關，台積電（2330）也飆218 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台經院景氣預測中心主任孫明德將台積電比喻為「大西瓜」，擔憂其過度成長吸走其他類股的養分，導致台股繁榮集中於特定科技大廠，發展不均衡。
• 孫明德預期台股指數有望挑戰4萬點，並指出此現象為全球資本市場趨勢，日本、韓國、中國及香港股市亦朝科技股發展。
• 他警示，若資源持續被單一巨頭壟斷，其他類股缺乏成長空間，將影響台股進一步衝刺的健康與韌性。
• 此新聞直接點名台積電，並由經濟預測機構提出對市場結構的擔憂，與公司營運及股價有高度關聯。
news.Keywords: 市場集中度, 產業結構, 科技巨頭, 成長風險, 估值擔憂

news.Title: High Oil Prices Are Driving an EV Boom. Who's the Next Winner After Tesla?
news.Content: Oil prices spiked when the U.S. attacked Iran and have been elevated since then as the Strait of Hormuz has remained closed. The effects of $4/gallon gasoline in the U.S, higher diesel prices, and even higher fuel prices in Europe, Asia, and other parts of the world are shifting demand patterns. Will AI create ...
news.AI_Summary:
• High oil prices are driving increased demand for Electric Vehicles (EVs), benefiting Tesla.
• Tesla reported Q1 automotive revenue up 16% to $16.2 billion, with deliveries surging over 150% quarter-over-quarter in parts of Europe.
• The company noted a positive impact on order rates from higher gas prices, with the Asian market also showing growth.
• Tesla's Q1 order backlog reached a two-year high.
• The article also mentions GE Vernova and Union Pacific as beneficiaries of high oil prices.
news.Keywords: EV demand, oil prices, automotive revenue, order backlog, market growth, energy transition

news.Title: ServiceNow Stock Just Got Slammed. Why I'm Still Not Buying the Dip.
news.Content: Shares of enterprise workflow software specialist ServiceNow (NYSE: NOW) dropped nearly 17% on Thursday, following the tech company 's earnings report. That is a disappointing move for a company that just beat the high end of its guidance and raised its full-year subscription revenue outlook. Even more, the company continued to highlight strong demand for its artificial intelligence (AI) -powered offerings. Will AI ...
news.AI_Summary:
• ServiceNow's shares dropped nearly 17% after its earnings report, despite beating high-end guidance and raising its full-year subscription revenue outlook.
• The company reported Q1 subscription revenue of $3.67 billion (+22% YoY) and total revenue of $3.77 billion (+22% YoY).
• Concerns about AI disrupting software pricing models were addressed by ServiceNow's hybrid pricing model, which includes usage-based components.
• The company's outlook includes headwinds from a recent acquisition, impacting gross and operating margins, and a projected step-down in cRPO growth.
• Despite strong organic business momentum, the stock's valuation at a P/E of around 50 is considered high given the risks.

news.Title: Intel's Earnings Report Shows How the CPU Has Found Its Way to the AI Boom.
news.Content: Shares of Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) surged in extended trading Thursday after the semiconductor company reported first-quarter results that were far better than Wall Street expected. For a company that has spent years trying to regain credibility with investors, this was an important update -- one that is driving home a new bull case for the stock. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel's Q1 revenue rose 7% year-over-year to $13.6 billion, significantly exceeding guidance.
• Adjusted EPS was $0.29, up from $0.13 in the prior year, and adjusted gross margin expanded to 41%.
• The data center and AI business showed strong growth, with revenue up 22% year-over-year to $5.1 billion, indicating acceleration.
• Intel's Q2 revenue outlook implies about 11% year-over-year growth.
• The article highlights the increasing importance of CPUs in AI infrastructure, not just GPUs, with Intel Xeon 6 selected for Nvidia's DGX Rubin systems.
news.Keywords: earnings, revenue, adjusted EPS, gross margin, data center, AI business, CPU, GPU

news.Title: Elon Musk vs. Plug Power Stock: Here's What the CEO of Tesla Thinks About Hydrogen Energy
news.Content: As far as I can tell, Elon Musk has never commented directly on Plug Power (NASDAQ: PLUG) stock. He has , however, commented plenty on the potential of hydrogen energy. After all, Musk is the CEO of Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) and SpaceX -- two businesses that are attempting to reimagine how the world creates and uses energy. Understanding how alternative fuels like hydrogen fit into the overall landscape ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Elon Musk has historically criticized hydrogen-powered cars, calling them 'extremely silly' and 'fool sells'.
• Tesla focuses on lithium-ion battery technology for its vehicles.
• The article suggests that while hydrogen may not be viable for cars, it could play a role in powering data center infrastructure.
• However, the cost competitiveness of hydrogen fuel remains a significant barrier to widespread adoption.
• Hydrogen stocks like Plug Power may rely on government regulations and subsidies.
news.Keywords: hydrogen energy, electric vehicles, battery technology, data centers, cost competitiveness, subsidies

news.Title: Where is Microsoft’s headquarters? Inside the tech giant’s corporate campus
news.Content: While Apple ( AAPL ) may be known for its spaceship-shaped Cupertino headquarters and Amazon ( AMZN ) operates out of three glass-enclosed spheres in Seattle, Microsoft ( MSFT ) distinguishes itself with a leafy green base that leans more “futuristic college” than sterile design showpiece. Its recently redesigned 500-acre campus boasts 125 buildings dedicated to collaboration, innovation, and sustainability — seamlessly nestled amongst forests, green spaces, and wildlife. The setting feels more like a wellness center than a tech hub and has ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft's headquarters are located in Redmond, Washington, on a 500-acre campus with 125 buildings.
• The campus design is described as 'futuristic college' and incorporates green spaces and wildlife.
• Microsoft has had three headquarters since its founding in 1975: Albuquerque, New Mexico; Bellevue, Washington; and Redmond, Washington.
• The Redmond campus houses approximately 50,000 employees and key divisions like Gaming, 365, AI, Cloud, and Windows.
• Microsoft allows hybrid work setups for its employees, with up to 2 days a week from home.
news.Keywords: headquarters, corporate campus, employee locations, hybrid work, AI divisions

news.Title: Tesla's Cybercab Pilot Production Is Underway. Is the Stock a Buy Ahead of a Potential Sales Surge?

• Active Full Self-Driving (Supervised) subscriptions increased to 1.28 million, and paid Robotaxi miles nearly doubled sequentially.
• Cybercab production ramp is expected to be a 'stretched-out S curve' with initial production being 'very slow'.
• Robotaxi revenue is not expected to be material this year but significant next year; stock trades at an extreme P/E ratio well above 300.

news.Title: Stock market today: Nasdaq and S&P 500 rise as Intel stock soars, Dow slips amid Hormuz tensions
news.Content: US stocks diverged on Friday as Intel ( INTC ) surged after earnings, lifting tech stocks, while investors weighed the implications of an extended Israel-Lebanon truce for the Hormuz standoff. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite ( ^IXIC ) climbed 0.6% as tech stocks showed some resilience amid the geopolitical uncertainty. The S&P 500 ( ^GSPC ) added 0.3%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average ( ^DJI ) slipped 0.1% following a losing day for Wall Street stocks. news.Title: Meta to lay off 10% of employees just days after announcing workplace tracking decision
news.Content: Earlier this week, social giant Meta Platforms made headlines after a memo to its employees said it would install software on employees' computers to train AI tools. Internally, it was understandably controversial, as there's no way to "opt out" of task tracking. Externally, it's likely to become a study for other companies thinking about doing something similar. But although the move likely runs aground of privacy regulations or stipulations in employment law, the undertones are obvious. After months of rumors ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta is planning to lay off 10% of its workforce (approximately 8,000 jobs) starting in May, with decisions landing on May 20.
• The company will also not fill 6,000 open roles, citing a continued effort to run the company more efficiently and offset AI investments.
• Meta's headcount nearly doubled from 2019 to 2022, and despite layoffs in 2023, it returned to growth in 2024 and 2025.
• The company's planned $600 billion investment in data centers is a significant factor, having burned through cash and tapped private credit.
news.Keywords: Meta layoffs, workforce reduction, AI investment, data centers, efficiency, headcount, capital expenditures, efficiency drive

That is now the case for Apple (AAPL) after reports came out that CEO Tim Cook will step down on Sept. 1 and be replaced by Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering John Ternus. news.Content: Investing.com -- Intel Corp on Thursday projected its second quarter revenue and profit well-above Wall Street estimates on strong demand for chipmaker’s server chips used in artificial-intelligence data centers. Shares rallied nearly 17% in after-market hours on Thursday. Intel said it sees ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel forecast Q2 revenue and profit above estimates, driven by strong demand for server chips used in AI data centers, causing shares to jump nearly 17%.
• Q2 EPS is projected at $0.20 (vs. consensus $0.09), and Q2 revenue is expected between $13.8-$14.8 billion (vs. consensus $13.04 billion).
• Intel's Q1 revenue was $13.6 billion (up 7% YoY), beating estimates, with the data center and AI segment revenue at $5.1 billion (up 22% YoY).

news.Title: Will Elon Musk's New SpaceX and Tesla Joint Venture Disrupt This AI Semiconductor Giant?
news.Content: Elon Musk appears to be on a path to combine all of his companies into a single entity. After a series of mergers, SpaceX, which launches rockets into space, is the parent company of X, the social media company formerly known as Twitter. It also includes the other operations of xAI, Musk's generative artificial intelligence (AI) lab. The latest effort to bring his companies closer together is a joint venture between SpaceX and Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) . Last month, Musk announced Terafab, a project that would dramatically expand global semiconductor supply if it reaches the scale Musk envisions. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Elon Musk's Terafab project aims to expand global semiconductor supply, potentially impacting Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC).
• Terafab is envisioned as a chip fab handling design, lithography, fabrication, packaging, and testing, including memory chip production.
• Musk plans for Terafab to produce 2nm chips at scale, requiring significant expertise and capital; Bernstein estimates $5 trillion for Musk's envisioned scale.
• Intel is partnering with the Terafab project, lending its design, printing, and packaging expertise, seeking a customer for its foundry business.
news.Keywords: Elon Musk, Terafab, semiconductor manufacturing, Tesla, SpaceX, Intel, TSMC, 2nm chips, foundry business, AI chips

news.Title: Why Meta's Layoffs, as Unfortunate as They Are, May Be a Buy Signal for the Stock
news.Content: Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) is planning to conduct another wave of layoffs, the company confirmed to media outlets this week. The company will cut about 10% of its global workforce -- or roughly 8,000 employees starting in May, with the potential for further reductions later in the year. The move comes as the company expects capital expenditures, fueled largely by artificial intelligence (AI) ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms plans to cut about 10% of its global workforce (roughly 8,000 employees) starting in May as part of an efficiency effort to offset heavy AI investments.
• The company forecast 2026 capital expenditures between $115 billion and $135 billion, requiring financial discipline to manage operational costs.
• Meta's revenue rose 24% year over year to $59.9 billion in Q4 2025, with management expecting growth to accelerate further.
• The company's demonstration of financial discipline, even through workforce reductions, could enhance the bull case for the stock.
news.Keywords: Meta Platforms, layoffs, AI investment, capital expenditures, efficiency, revenue growth, financial discipline, workforce reduction, operational costs

news.Title: Musk sounds cautious tone on robotaxis amid slower-than-expected rollout
news.Content: By Chris Kirkham and Abhirup Roy LOS ANGELES, April 23 (Reuters) - The rollout of Tesla ’s fledgling robotaxi business is going more slowly than expected, several Wall Street analysts said, following an uncharacteristically downbeat update from CEO Elon Musk. Musk over the past year has outlined an aggressive expansion plan for ‌Tesla’s autonomous ride-hailing operation. During Tesla’s first-quarter earnings call Wednesday, he struck a more cautious tone. More from Yahoo Scout What challenges is Tesla facing ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's robotaxi business rollout is slower than expected, with CEO Elon Musk adopting a more cautious tone.
• Musk hopes to have robotaxis in a dozen states by year-end, taking a 'cautious approach' to avoid injuries.
• Analysts note Tesla is likely contending with the ground game of launching self-driving vehicles at scale.
• Musk stated robotaxis will likely 'not be super material this year,' but 'will be material probably in a significant way next year.'

news.Title: Is Arm Stock a Buy at New All-Time Highs?
news.Content: British semiconductor juggernaut Arm Holdings plc (ARM) reached an all-time high of $210.80 today, and is only slightly down from that level. The stock reached this level after a six-day bull run on Wall Street (as of the writing of this article), with a 12% intraday gain on April 22. The stock is facing heightened demand for semiconductors as the artificial intelligence (AI) boom keeps a strict hold on the market. Investors are also looking forward to its upcoming earnings ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arm Holdings plc reached an all-time high of $210.80, driven by heightened demand for semiconductors amid the AI boom.
• The company's Q3 fiscal 2026 revenue increased 26% YoY to $1.24 billion, with licensing revenue up 25% and royalty revenue up 27%.
• Non-GAAP operating income grew 14% YoY to $505 million, and non-GAAP EPS surged 10% to $0.43.

news.Title: Wall Street Can’t Seem to Get Enough of Intel Stock
news.Content: Popular chip giant Intel Corporation (INTC) is rising after favorable analyst sentiments. Notably, the stock has risen more than 200% over the past year, as the demand from data centers and server CPUs propelled it higher. Moreover, Intel’s foundry progress, plus geopolitical shifts favoring U.S. manufacturing, has rebuilt investor confidence. The company is also facing significant tailwinds from agentic AI. • Intel's 14A chip manufacturing technology is expected to be used by Tesla for its Terafab project.

news.Title: Why American Express Fell Today
news.Content: Shares of credit card giant American Express (NYSE: AXP) fell on Thursday, down 4.6% as of 2:16 p.m. EDT. American Express released its first-quarter earnings report this morning, and the results appeared quite strong, conflicting with the negative price action. Will AI create the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• American Express (AXP) shares fell 4.6% despite reporting Q1 revenue of $18.9 billion (up 11.4%) and EPS of $4.28 (up 17.6%), both beating analyst expectations.
• Management reiterated full-year guidance of 9-10% revenue growth and $17.30-$17.90 EPS, planning to increase marketing and technology investments.
• Investors may have preferred increased share repurchases or dividends over additional spending, potentially on AI.
• The stock now trades at around 18 times forward earnings estimates, at the lower end of its historical range.
news.Keywords: American Express, AXP, Q1 earnings, revenue, EPS, guidance, capital expenditures, AI investment, stock valuation, market sentiment

news.Title: This Stock May Be the Simplest Way to Double Your Money by 2027
news.Content: The year may not have been a great one for the stock market so far, primarily due to the Middle East crisis, which has led to an increase in volatility, higher oil prices, and a growing probability of a recession. But not all stocks have been bogged down by the broader market's weakness. The Nasdaq-100 index has appreciated 5% in 2026. However, Sandisk (NASDAQ: ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Sandisk (SNDK) stock has jumped 285% year-to-date, driven by massive demand for its flash storage chips used in AI data centers.
• NAND flash prices rose by 246% in 2025, triggering demand for enterprise solid-state drives (SSDs) as AI data centers exhausted hard-disk drive supply.
• Flash memory manufacturers like Kioxia have sold out their 2026 and expect to sell out 2027 capacity, indicating sustained high NAND flash pricing.
• Gartner projects a 234% spike in NAND flash prices for 2026, with potential for triple-digit increases next year due to memory shortages extending into 2028.
news.Keywords: Sandisk, SNDK, stock performance, NAND flash, AI data centers, SSD demand, memory shortage, price growth, Gartner forecast, fiscal 2026

news.Content: Earnings growth is essential for any organization, regardless of size, because profitability determines long-term survival. Earnings are calculated by subtracting the cost of producing goods and services from a company’s revenues over a period. Earnings strongly influence share prices, with earnings expectations playing a major role. On that note, ...

• NVIDIA (NVDA) and Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) are showing strong earnings growth, with NVIDIA's expected growth rate at nearly 69% and Broadcom's at 67.9% for the current year.
• Both companies have a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy) for NVIDIA and #1 (Strong Buy) for Broadcom.
• Screening criteria included strong historical EPS growth, year-over-year earnings growth of 5%+, and positive estimate revisions.
• These two stocks were the only ones identified by the Research Wizard screening tool based on the specified parameters.

news.Title: Cisco Cashes in on AI Order Momentum: More Upside Ahead in 2026?
news.Content: Cisco Systems CSCO is witnessing a sharp acceleration in AI-related demand, especially from hyperscalers. AI orders in second-quarter fiscal 2026 increased to $2.1 billion from $1.3 billion in first-quarter fiscal 2026, which is equivalent to the entire fiscal 2025 AI orders. The AI order for fiscal 2026 has been pegged at more than $5 billion, which is a sign of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Cisco Systems (CSCO) is experiencing accelerated AI-related demand, particularly from hyperscalers, with AI orders reaching $2.1 billion in Q2 fiscal 2026.
• AI orders for fiscal 2026 are pegged at over $5 billion, indicating a step-function acceleration.
• Hyperscaler demand drove 65% YoY growth in service provider and cloud orders, with hyperscaler demand showing triple-digit growth.
• Cisco is deepening strategic partnerships with NVIDIA (up 70% sequentially) and AMD to expand its total addressable market in the AI ecosystem.
news.Keywords: Cisco Systems, CSCO, AI demand, hyperscalers, AI orders, NVIDIA partnership, AMD partnership, networking solutions, fiscal 2026 forecast, AI ecosystem

news.Title: Alphabet Has a Massive Advantage in the AI Race -- and No, It's Not Gemini
news.Content: When investors talk about artificial intelligence (AI), they usually focus on models. Who has the smartest system? Who leads on benchmarks? Who launches the most impressive features? That focus makes sense. Better models often translate into better products. But it misses a more important question: Who can afford to keep building, improving, and scaling AI over ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (GOOGL) holds a significant advantage in the AI race due to its massive profitability and $165 billion in operating cash flow in 2025.
• This financial strength allows Alphabet to invest aggressively in AI infrastructure, including custom chips and data center expansion, without compromising stability.
• Alphabet expects to spend approximately $180 billion in capital expenditures (capex) in 2026.
• Time is a strategic advantage for Alphabet, enabling it to endure early inefficiencies, refine products, and absorb short-term trade-offs, unlike newer AI companies.
news.Keywords: Alphabet, GOOGL, AI race, profitability, cash flow, capital expenditures, AI infrastructure, custom chips, data centers, strategic advantage

news.Title: Apple gets a stunning boost as smartphone rivals stumble
news.Content: Apple's ( AAPL ) most recent victory in China is worth more than simply bragging rights about smartphones, CNBC reported . New market data show that the company's iPhone shipments in China rose 20% in the first quarter, the fastest growth among major suppliers, even while the country's overall smartphone market decreased 4% . Investors can't ignore that kind of difference, especially since China has been one of Apple's major worries in the market over the past year. The timing is important. Apple will release its fiscal second-quarter results on April 30, 2026 . The business had a great Christmas ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple's iPhone shipments in China rose 20% in Q1, the fastest growth among major suppliers, while the overall Chinese smartphone market decreased 4%.
• Apple will release its fiscal Q2 results on April 30, following a Christmas quarter with record sales of $143.8 billion (up 16% YoY) and record iPhone sales of $85.3 billion.
• Gaining market share in a declining market while achieving record profits suggests Apple is entering earnings season with strong momentum.
• Chinese buyers view Apple devices as having value due to their longevity, even as competitors raise prices to cover memory costs.
news.Keywords: Apple, AAPL, China market share, iPhone shipments, Q1 results, record sales, smartphone market, earnings momentum, product longevity, AI integration

news.Title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 with improved coding and research capabilities
news.Content: Investing.com -- OpenAI announced Thursday the release of GPT-5.5, its latest AI model now available to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users through ChatGPT and Codex platforms. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• OpenAI released GPT-5.5, achieving 82.7% accuracy on Terminal-Bench 2.0 and 58.6% on SWE-Bench Pro.
• GPT-5.5 maintains similar latency to GPT-5.4 but uses fewer tokens; it was developed using NVIDIA GB200 and GB300 NVL72 systems.
• For professional applications, GPT-5.5 scored 84.9% on GDPval and 80.5% on OSWorld-Verified.
• API pricing for GPT-5.5 is $5/million input tokens and $30/million output tokens, with a 1-million token context window.
news.Keywords: OpenAI, GPT-5.5, AI model, accuracy, benchmark, NVIDIA, GB200, GB300, API pricing, context window

news.Title: Lam Research Q3 Earnings Call Highlights
news.Content: Key Points Lam reported March-quarter revenue of $5.84 billion (up 9% sequentially, 24% YoY) and beat EPS guidance, and management raised 2026 WFE spending expectations to $140 billion with a bias to the upside as AI-driven semiconductor demand accelerates while noting near-term execution constraints. Memory and foundry strength drove mix shifts—Foundry was 54% of systems revenue while Memory was 39% with record DRAM contribution—and the Customer Support Business Group posted a record $2.1 billion quarter as Lam expanded productivity tools like Equipment Intelligence and Dextro cobots. Profitability improved (gross margin 49.9%) and Lam returned significant capital including roughly $800 million ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Lam Research reported Q1 2026 revenue of $5.84 billion (up 9% sequentially, 24% YoY), beating EPS guidance, and raised 2026 WFE spending expectations to $140 billion.
• Foundry represented 54% of systems revenue, while Memory was 39%, driven by record DRAM contribution; Customer Support Business Group posted a record $2.1 billion quarter.
• Profitability improved with gross margin at 49.9%, and Lam returned significant capital, including $800 million in buybacks and dividends.
• The company guided Q2 revenue of $6.6 billion ± $400 million and gross margin ~50.5%, expecting a second Malaysia manufacturing facility to start in H2.
news.Keywords: Lam Research, LRCX, Q1 earnings, revenue, EPS, WFE spending, AI demand, Foundry, Memory, Customer Support, gross margin, guidance

news.Title: Nvidia Just Deployed the Nuclear Option
news.Content: Nuclear energy is making a comeback in the U.S. in a big way. Fossil fuels contribute to global warming, and solar and wind energy face limitations. Add to that the rapid adoption of energy-hungry artificial intelligence (AI) data centers, and you have a perfect storm driving demand for nuclear power. It's a bit ironic, then, that Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) , whose graphics processing units (GPUs) underpin ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia is partnering with nuclear start-up Oklo and Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL) to advance nuclear energy solutions for AI infrastructure.
• The collaboration aims to develop AI models for nuclear fuel validation and research materials and fabrication of plutonium-based fuels.
• Oklo also has a deal with Meta Platforms to provide power for its data centers, indicating a broader trend of AI companies securing energy solutions.
• Nuclear energy is experiencing a renaissance, with Bank of America projecting a $10 trillion opportunity due to surging electricity demand and its reliable baseload power.
news.Keywords: Nvidia, NVDA, Oklo, LANL, nuclear energy, AI infrastructure, nuclear fuel, Meta Platforms, data centers, Bank of America, nuclear power

news.Title: Why Texas Instruments Rocketed Higher Today
news.Content: Shares of Texas Instruments (NASDAQ: TXN) rallied 19.5% on Thursday as of 12:38 p.m. EDT, sending shares to a new all-time high. The embedded and analog semiconductor sector, which Texas Instruments leads, has been in a longer-than-usual downturn since the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Texas Instruments (TXN) shares rallied 19.5% to a new all-time high following an outstanding Q1 earnings report and forecast.
• Q1 revenue grew 18.7% to $4.83 billion, and EPS rallied 31.3% to $1.68 per share, both handily beating expectations.
• Management guided for continued strength in Q2, projecting revenue of $5.0-$5.4 billion and EPS of $1.77-$2.05, exceeding analyst estimates.
• The company is benefiting from rebounding industrial and auto sectors, as well as increased use of analog, embedded, and power chips in AI data centers.
news.Keywords: Texas Instruments, TXN, stock rally, all-time high, Q1 earnings, Q2 forecast, industrial demand, data center demand, AI, semiconductor cycle

news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . SoftBank ( SFTBY ) is leaning even harder into its AI ...

• SoftBank is reportedly looking to raise $10 billion through a margin loan backed by its OpenAI stake to further its AI strategy.

news.Title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs, Microsoft offers buyouts to to staff as AI spending costs hit Big Tech workers
news.Content: Meta ( META ) on Thursday became the latest Big Tech company to announce layoffs as the ballooning costs of the AI build-out and efficiencies continue to hit workers in the industry. The company says it will cut 10% of its workforce, or about 8,000 employees. The news comes after a trickle of reports suggested the company ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta (META) announced layoffs of 10% of its workforce (approx. 8,000 employees) due to ballooning AI build-out costs and efficiency drives.
• Microsoft (MSFT) will offer voluntary buyouts to roughly 7% of its US employees (senior directors and below) if age plus years of service equals 70 or more.
• Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOG, GOOGL), Meta, and Oracle (ORCL) are all spending billions on data centers and AI models, with combined 2026 capex estimated at $650 billion.
• Microsoft stock is down about 15% year-to-date, while Meta stock is roughly flat.
news.Keywords: Meta layoffs, Microsoft buyouts, AI spending, Big Tech, capital expenditures, workforce reduction, data centers, tech stocks, stock performance

news.Title: Why ServiceNow Stock Is Plummeting Today

news.AI_Summary:

• This report is seen as a referendum on SaaS stocks amid AI disruption concerns, with the margin guidance potentially signaling weakening pricing power.
• The market may be overreacting to the guidance, as the company's subscription revenue growth is projected between 21-21.5%.

news.Title: Intel’s $250 Billion Rally Slams Into a Potential Earnings Wall
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Intel Corp. has been one of the hottest stocks in the market over the past 12 months, soaring 235% to the highest price since the dot-com bubble. But the rally is facing a potential roadblock in the company’s first-quarter earnings report due after the close Thursday. news.AI_Summary:
• Intel's stock has soared 235% over the past 12 months, reaching its highest price since the dot-com bubble.
• Analysts expect Q1 adjusted EPS of 1 cent (a 92% drop YoY) and a slight revenue decline to $12.4 billion.
• Gross margins are projected to fall to less than 35% from 39% in Q1 2025.

news.Title: Prediction: Apple Stock Will Flourish Under New CEO
news.Content: For the first time in 15 years, Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) is set to get a new voice at the top, with Tim Cook stepping down as CEO in September 2026 and transitioning to executive chairman. While the news came as a surprise, I think it will be good for ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tim Cook will step down as Apple CEO in September 2026, becoming executive chairman; John Ternus, SVP of Hardware Engineering, will succeed him.
• Cook oversaw Apple's growth to a trillion-dollar market cap and the introduction of Apple Watch and AirPods.
• Ternus, a 'tech guy' compared to Cook's 'operations guy' background, may bring renewed product innovation, particularly in AI strategy.

news.Title: Will 8th-Generation TPUs Move the Needle for Google Stock?
news.Content: The AI buildout around Alphabet's Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) ecosystem is still picking up speed. Broadcom (AVGO) is deepening its long-term partnership with Google to make advanced chips, including hardware tied to Google TPUs, while Anthropic has lined up about 3.5 gigawatts of computing capacity tied to Google’s processors. On top of that, Alphabet’s 2026 capital spending could reach $175–185 billion, with a big chunk going into AI-focused data centers, which will ripple through both chip suppliers and the cloud side of the business. More News from Barchart Broadcom Breakup: AVGO Stock Slumps as Google ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet's Google is deepening its partnership with Broadcom for advanced chips, including those tied to Google TPUs.
• Alphabet's 2026 capital spending could reach $175-$185 billion, with a significant portion allocated to AI-focused data centers.
• Google unveiled its eighth-generation TPUs (TPU 8t for training, TPU 8i for inference) promising better performance-per-dollar.
• Google Cloud revenue grew 48% YoY in Q4 2025, with an annual run rate exceeding $70 billion.
• Alphabet's stock has risen 118.64% over the past 12 months, trading at a forward P/E of 28.81 times, double the sector average.

news.Title: Why the Best Time to Buy Artificial Intelligence (AI) Growth Stocks Is When Everyone Else Is Rotating Out of Tech
news.Content: The artificial intelligence (AI) megatrend has been the primary driver of an incredible bull market over the three or so years. That should be no surprise to most investors, particularly those who have seen their investments in AI juggernauts like Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) go through the roof. But these stocks hit a wall late last year and spent the first quarter of 2026 mostly going backward. There were several reasons why AI-related stocks in particular lost ground, starting with ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI stocks have seen significant returns, but some valuations are high relative to financial growth, with concerns about massive infrastructure spending.
• The recent correction in AI stocks was driven by high P/E ratios and worries about capex not paying off, leading investors to rotate into value and dividend stocks.
• The Iran war added market uncertainty, causing the Nasdaq Composite to enter a correction, but tech stocks have since recovered.
• Investors are returning to AI stocks as valuations have fallen, recognizing AI as transformative technology with long-term value potential.
news.Keywords: AI stocks, valuations, P/E ratio, capital expenditures, market correction, rotation, Iran war, Nasdaq Composite, tech stocks, transformative technology

news.Title: Cadence Expands TSMC Alliance to Boost Advanced AI Chip Design
news.Content: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. CDNS has deepened its long-standing collaboration with TSMC to accelerate innovation in AI-driven semiconductor design, reinforcing its leadership in enabling next-generation silicon. This expanded partnership focuses on delivering silicon-proven IP, signoff-ready end-to-end design infrastructure and certified flows for advanced AI chips built on TSMC’s most cutting-edge process technologies, including N3, N2, A16 and A14. By aligning closely with TSMC’s roadmap, Cadence is helping customers reduce design iterations, improve correlation and achieve faster, more predictable paths to silicon—critical advantages in an era defined ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) has deepened its collaboration with TSMC to accelerate AI-driven semiconductor design, focusing on advanced process technologies (N3, N2, A16, A14).
• The partnership aims to reduce design iterations and improve correlation, supporting Cadence's 'Design for AI and AI for Design' strategy.
• Cadence is delivering a comprehensive IP portfolio optimized for TSMC nodes, including HBM4E, and its Artisan foundation IP is deployed in production designs on TSMC's 3nm technologies.
• The integration of agentic AI across Cadence's platforms, like Genus Synthesis and Innovus Implementation, enhances optimization for TSMC's advanced architectures.
news.Keywords: Cadence Design Systems, CDNS, TSMC, AI design, semiconductor, process technologies, IP portfolio, agentic AI, EDA, HBM4E

news.Title: Apple Is Getting a New Chief Hardware Officer. What Does That Mean for AAPL Stock?
news.Content: Apple (AAPL) stock has been in a steady uptrend with returns of 36.76% in the last 52 weeks. On Monday, the company appointed Johny Srouji as chief hardware officer, effective immediately. Notably, Srouji has been working with a team of silicon and technology engineers, with focus ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple appointed Johny Srouji as chief hardware officer, a move Oppenheimer analysts called 'incrementally positive' for the company's AI strategy.
• Srouji will lead the team developing in-house chips, potentially reducing Apple's reliance on Intel, Qualcomm, and Broadcom.
• The leadership change, with John Ternus succeeding Tim Cook as CEO, may bring renewed product innovation and a bolder approach to AI.
• Apple's M5 chip, launched in Oct 2025, offers enhanced AI performance for devices like MacBook Pro and iPad Pro.
news.Keywords: Apple, AAPL, Johny Srouji, chief hardware officer, AI strategy, in-house chips, John Ternus, CEO, M5 chip, hardware innovation

news.Title: Amazon Deepens AI Push With Expanded Anthropic Deal: What's Ahead?
news.Content: Amazon AMZN is deepening its generative-AI footprint after announcing an expanded strategic collaboration with Anthropic on April 20, 2026. Under the agreement, Anthropic has committed to spending more than $100 billion on AWS technologies over the next decade, covering Trainium2, Trainium3, Trainium4 and future generations of Amazon's custom silicon, along with tens of millions of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon (AMZN) deepened its generative AI footprint by expanding its collaboration with Anthropic, committing over $100 billion to AWS technologies over the next decade.
• Anthropic will secure up to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity, with meaningful Trainium2 capacity coming online in H1 2026 and nearly 1 GW by year-end.

news.Title: Tesla Q1 analyst reaction: Capex rise a surprise but needed for AI, Optimus, robotaxi, chip fab build-out
news.Content: Though Tesla ( TSLA ) stock is lower following the release of its first quarter earnings report , Wall Street analysts are generally encouraged, though cautious. Tesla’s larger-than-expected $25 billion capital expenditures guide caught many off guard, as it was more than investors expected. “TSLA would be moving higher after-hours, instead of trading sideways, if not for a $5B increase in the 2026 capex outlook,” Piper Sandler analyst Alexander Potter wrote on Wednesday night. “The previous outlook (of $20B) was already around 2x higher than Tesla's previous peak, and the new outlook (of $25B) illuminates the scale of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's Q1 earnings showed a $25 billion+ capital expenditure plan for 2026, a significant increase from previous forecasts, to accelerate AI, robotics, and expansion.
• The company expects negative free cash flow for the rest of 2026 due to these investments, despite a $1.44 billion Q1 surplus.
• Analysts expressed caution, noting that the capex increase highlights Tesla's ambitious scale but implies strongly negative free cash flow.
• While Tesla's AI investments are 'bearing fruit' with FSD subscriptions up, the robotaxi rollout is 'slow and steady,' with meaningful revenue not expected until 2027.
news.Keywords: Tesla, capital expenditures, AI, robotics, robotaxi, FSD, negative free cash flow, Elon Musk, investor faith, manufacturing expansion

news.Title: Why Oracle Stock Just Dropped
news.Content: Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) stock sank 6% through 10:05 a.m. after news broke this morning of a falling-out the company may be having with key server supplier Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ: SMCI) . ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Oracle (ORCL) stock sank 6% after reportedly canceling an order for 300-400 Super Micro server racks with Nvidia chips.
• Pundits suggest Oracle canceled to distance itself from accusations of illicit Nvidia chip sales to China by Super Micro's co-founder.
• Another possible reason is Oracle feeling overextended in AI and wanting to reduce risk if costs rise and revenue growth slows.
• Super Micro could lose $1.05 billion to $1.4 billion in sales from the canceled order, though some racks were already shipped.
news.Keywords: Oracle, ORCL, Super Micro, SMCI, Nvidia, server racks, stock drop, China sales, AI spending, risk reduction, lost sales

news.Title: Tesla Just Shocked Wall Street With $22.4 Billion in Revenue. Here Are 3 Other Takeaways From the EV Giant's Latest Quarterly Results.
news.Content: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) stock initially spiked after it reported first-quarter results. At first glance, that made a lot of sense. Investors saw earnings that handily beat expectations, driven by 16% year-over-year revenue growth. That $22.4 billion in sales disappointed Wall Street observers, though, who expected $22.6 billion. What really shocked Wall Street was more on the other side of the ledger. Capital spending plans soared. That points to CEO Elon Musk's plan to effectively turn the electric vehicle (EV) maker into an artificial intelligence (AI) hub. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's Q1 results showed $22.4 billion in sales, slightly missing Wall Street's $22.6 billion expectation, but EPS of $0.41 beat the $0.37 estimate.
• Capital spending plans soared, with projections exceeding $25 billion for 2026, indicating a shift towards becoming an AI hub.
• Profitability was boosted by one-time benefits and higher selling prices, but investors need to assess future potential beyond the current EV business.
• Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot and robotaxi fleet are key future revenue drivers, but robotaxi revenue is not expected to be substantial until 2027.
news.Keywords: Tesla, Q1 results, sales miss, EPS beat, capital spending, AI hub, Optimus, robotaxi, future revenue, EV business

news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Microsoft ( NASDAQ:MSFT ) is making a big move in Australia, committing $18 billion ...

記者陳建興∕台北報導

news.Content: Most artificial intelligence (AI) stocks have skyrocketed over the past several years, helping to push the Nasdaq-100 , which is dominated by tech stocks, up more than 100% over the past three years. While many of the returns are warranted, as tech companies have seen their sales and earnings grow quickly over this period, some AI stocks are running hot right now, and not all have the financial growth to justify their rapid share price ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Many AI stocks have seen significant gains, but some valuations may not be justified by financial growth, with concerns about high infrastructure spending.
• Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) is experiencing rapid sales and earnings growth due to its dominant position in manufacturing advanced AI processors.
• TSMC produces 70% of global processors and holds an estimated 90% market share for advanced processors (3nm and 4nm).
• In Q1 2026, sales rose 41% to $35 billion, and net income increased 58% to $18.1 billion, with management projecting 30% sales growth in 2026.
news.Keywords: AI stocks, valuations, infrastructure spending, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, TSM, TSMC, AI processors, semiconductor manufacturing, sales growth, net income

news.Title: Here's Why Nvidia and Broadcom Are Still Leading the Pack for AI Investing
news.Content: There are countless ways to invest in artificial intelligence. While some may prefer to put money into software companies or electrical infrastructure suppliers, the way I keep circling back to is through the chip designers, notably Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) and Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) . Both of these are seen as premier AI investments, and they can be summed up with one word: profits. These two companies are ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) are leading AI investments due to their strong profitability and expected earnings growth.
• Nvidia's GPUs have high demand, with revenue growing 73% to $68.1 billion in its latest quarter, and analysts expect continued strong growth.
• Broadcom designs custom AI chips (ASICs) tailored for specific workloads, experiencing 106% YoY growth in its AI semiconductor division.
• Both companies are positioned for long-term benefits from AI, with Nvidia expecting global data center capex to rise through 2030.
news.Keywords: Nvidia, NVDA, Broadcom, AVGO, AI investments, profitability, earnings growth, GPUs, custom AI chips, ASICs, data center capex

news.Title: Why the Fed's Next Move Could Be the Most Important Catalyst for Stocks This Spring
news.Content: Oil prices have surged over the past two months, as world leaders fail to resolve the war in Iran. The impasse has resulted in higher gas prices for consumers, with the national average at $4.02 per gallon, according to AAA. It's also led to higher overall inflation. Plastic products, fertilizers, and other items that use oil in the manufacturing process are now more expensive to produce, and pricier fuel drives up shipping costs. Will AI create the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Oil prices have surged due to the war in Iran, leading to higher inflation (3.3% YoY) and increased pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.
• Fed Chair Jerome Powell is taking a 'wait and see' approach but acknowledges the tension between controlling inflation and supporting economic growth.
• Raising interest rates could slow economic growth and increase unemployment.
• Typically, lower interest rates benefit the stock market, but sustained inflation could force the Fed to raise rates, potentially causing a stock market drop.
• Investors are advised to maintain a long-term outlook amidst market uncertainty.
news.Keywords: oil prices, inflation, Federal Reserve, interest rates, economic growth, unemployment, stock market

news.AI_Summary:
• The crypto market is dominated by Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH), which command a majority of the sector's market cap.
• Bitcoin serves as a scarce store of value, with significant institutional and corporate adoption.
• Ethereum hosts the majority of DeFi value locked ($45.3 billion) and tokenized real-world assets.
• Diversifying beyond BTC and ETH into altcoins is considered speculative and prone to poor performance during drawdowns.
• An 80/20 split favoring Bitcoin is suggested for portfolio allocation due to its lower volatility and institutional bid.

news.Title: Now Even Volvo Is Putting Tesla in the Rearview Mirror?
news.Content: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) investors have been along for a bumpy ride over the last 18 months or so as they've seen competitors pull ahead of the automaker that helped reenergize the global electric vehicle (EV) industry. BYD , China's juggernaut EV maker, raced ahead of Tesla in global EV sales. A handful of companies have expanded their robotaxi ambitions beyond Tesla's current scope, even considering its recent expansion. And now, even Volvo is putting some distance between itself and Tesla, with Coca-Cola ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla faces increasing competition in the EV market, with BYD surpassing it in global sales and Volvo expanding its electric semi-truck presence.
• Volvo has deployed over 5,000 electric semi-trucks, logging over 100 million zero-emission miles, while Tesla has 'a few hundred' in pilot programs.
• Tesla's volume semi production is set to begin in H1 2026 at its Nevada factory with a capacity of 50,000 trucks annually.
• Success for Tesla's semi in the early stages would be capturing a few thousand trucks in the first couple of years, comparable to Volvo's current deployment.

news.Title: UBS adjusts price target on Alphabet stock
news.Content: UBS likes what it sees at Alphabet. It's just not ready to say "buy." The Swiss bank raised its price target on Alphabet ( GOOGL ) to $375 from $348 this week, a meaningful revision driven by key factors. Key factors include the accelerating Google Cloud growth, easing fears about ChatGPT eroding search ad revenue, and a potentially lucrative new business hiding inside the company's AI chip division. The neutral rating, however, stayed exactly where it was. That combination, higher target and unchanged rating, is the tension worth unpacking. UBS is effectively telling investors that the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• UBS raised its price target on Alphabet (GOOGL) to $375 from $348, citing accelerating Google Cloud growth, easing ChatGPT concerns for search ads, and potential in AI chips.
• Despite the higher target, UBS maintained a neutral rating, suggesting the current stock price already reflects near-term momentum.
• Google Cloud revenue hit $17.66 billion in Q4 2025 (+48% YoY), with an annual run rate over $70 billion, driven by AI product demand.
• Alphabet unveiled its eighth-generation TPU chipset, including a version optimized for AI inference.
• Google Search revenue surged 17% YoY in Q4 2025, with AI integration credited for higher query volumes and sales lifts for advertisers.
news.Keywords: price target, neutral rating, Google Cloud, AI chips, TPU, search ads, revenue growth, valuation

news.Title: Tesla Earnings Highlight Soaring Full Self-Driving (Supervised) Subscriptions. Here's a Look at This Catalyst -- and 3 More Surprising Figures From the Report
news.Content: Shares of Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) moved higher in extended trading on Wednesday after the electric vehicle company released its first-quarter update, and it's not hard to see why. Yes, total revenue rose 16% year over year to $22.39 billion. Yes, free cash flow came in at a surprisingly strong $1.44 billion. But the more important part of the report may have been what management said about Tesla's attempt to become something more than a car company. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly," providing the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's Q1 revenue rose 16% YoY to $22.39 billion, with free cash flow at $1.44 billion.
• Active Full Self-Driving (Supervised) subscriptions soared to 1.28 million (+51% YoY), indicating monetization per vehicle is growing faster than volume.
• Services and other revenue surged 42% YoY to $3.75 billion, now accounting for nearly 17% of total revenue.

news.Title: Mark Zuckerberg sends shocking message to Meta employees
news.Content: Most tech companies talk abstractly about building AI from human behavior. Meta just sent its employees a memo making it concrete, and personal. The company is installing tracking software on employee computers. It will record mouse movements, keystrokes, clicks, and screenshots. And workers cannot opt out. The message behind the move is hard to misread. Meta is telling its own workforce that their daily behavior is now training data. What Meta is actually doing According to a memo sent to staff and seen by Reuters , Meta is rolling out a tool called the Model Capability Initiative. The software runs ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta is installing tracking software on employee computers to record mouse movements, keystrokes, clicks, and screenshots for AI training.
• Employees cannot opt out of the 'Model Capability Initiative' tool, which captures real-time computer interactions.
• Meta stated the data is needed for AI agents to learn how people use computers and will not be used beyond AI training.
• The absence of an opt-out option raises concerns about consent, morale, and workplace trust.
• The initiative is tied to Meta's AI restructuring and the acquisition of a 49% stake in data-labeling firm Scale AI.
news.Keywords: employee tracking, AI training, data collection, consent, morale, workplace trust, AI agents

news.Title: How many employees does Microsoft have in 2026? MSFT’s workforce, locations & layoffs explained
news.Content: Bill Gates may be the iconic face of Microsoft ( MSFT ), but he isn’t solely responsible for the company’s groundbreaking achievements — those were driven by a global team of highly skilled and dedicated workers. news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft employs 228,000 full-time workers (125,000 in the US, 103,000 international) as of its 2025 Annual Report.
• The company invested $88 billion in AI in fiscal year 2025, leading to significant workforce cuts (15,000 jobs, including 9,000 in July).
• Despite record performance in 2025 (revenue up 15% to $281.7 billion, operating income up 17%), Microsoft is streamlining its workforce to offset AI investments.

news.Title: Tesla takes big swing with new model in key market
news.Content: Tesla is struggling mightily in India, reportedly selling just 350 Model Y's in India since it debuted last year, according to Reuters. But now the company is debuting a new version of the Model Y to help goose sales in the burgeoning EV market. Pricing due to heavy tariffs means Tesla still has a lot of work to do to succeed on the subcontinent, but CEO Elon Musk arguably isn't making it easy for his company. Last year, as Tesla was looking to break into the Indian market, Musk got into a back-and-forth of insults ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla is struggling in India, selling only 350 Model Ys since its debut, and faces high tariffs on imported vehicles.
• The company launched a new six-seater version of the Model Y in India, priced around Rs 6.199 crore ($66,000 USD).
• This price is higher than competitors like BYD's Sealion 7 ($53,000-$59,000 USD) in India.
• India aims to increase EV share to 30% by 2030 but did not make concessions on EV tariffs in a recent trade agreement with the U.S.
• Tesla has lobbied the Indian government for years to lower EV tariffs but has been told to build vehicles in India, a plan it recently scrapped.
news.Keywords: India market, EV sales, tariffs, Model Y, competition, trade agreement, production plans

news.Title: Google Unveils 2 New AI Chips to Take on Nvidia
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) made a name for itself by pioneering the graphics processing units (GPUs) that became the gold standard for rendering images in video games. The company adapted these chips to handle the rigors of artificial intelligence (AI) , a field it now dominates. Unfortunately, being the leader means there's always someone trying to take you down. Alphabet 's (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Google unveiled two new AI chips: the eighth-generation TPU 8t for training and TPU 8i for inference.
• The TPU 8t offers 3x compute performance, 10x faster storage access, and double the chip data transfer rate compared to its predecessor.
• The TPU 8i features high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and 3x SRAM, reducing latency for AI agents, and includes a custom Arm-based Axiom CPU.
• These innovations deliver 80% better performance-per-dollar, offering customers twice the volume for the same cost.
• Alphabet remains reliant on Nvidia's GPUs but aims to decrease reliance through its custom TPUs, offering more affordable AI processing options.
news.Keywords: AI chips, TPU, Google, Nvidia, performance-per-dollar, AI inference, AI training, custom silicon

news.Title: What's Going on With Micron's Stock?
news.Content: Micron (NASDAQ: MU) investors have had some serious whiplash over the past few months. Earlier this year, the stock set a new all-time high, capping off a massive rally as investors dove headfirst into the stock to take advantage of the memory chip shortage. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron (MU) stock has experienced volatility, rallying to an all-time high, crashing 30%, and then returning to all-time highs within a month.
• The memory chip shortage is a key driver, with Micron estimating it can meet only half to two-thirds of HBM demand, causing prices to soar.
• The HBM market is projected to triple from $35 billion in 2025 to $100 billion in 2028.
• Google's TurboQuant algorithm, which reduces memory usage, could potentially alleviate the supply issue, but the overall crunch persists.
• Micron's stock has massive upside potential if the supply shortage continues for years, but could sell off heavily once resolved.
news.Keywords: stock volatility, memory chip shortage, HBM demand, AI, supply crunch, price surge, Google algorithm, upside potential
